raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased laboratory equipment on XXXX using my Citi credit card. The item was listed as functional but was not working properly and did not match the description. 

The item was sold under XXXX  Used condition, which states that items are fully operational, and the seller made specific functional claims, further supporting that this was not buyers remorse but a legitimate return attempt. 

The item was picked up on XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Money Back Guarantee states that buyers have 30 calendar days after delivery to request a return. I submitted my return request on XX/XX/year>, which is the XXXX calendar day after pickup. 

XXXX denied the return based on an undisclosed internal rule that the 30-day return window expires at the exact pickup timestamp, rather than at the end of the XXXX calendar day. This restriction is not disclosed in XXXX written policy. XXXX also refused to explain in writing how my request was outside the stated 30-day window. 

I disputed the charge with Citi for goods not as described and refusal of return within the stated timeframe. Citi denied the dispute and reversed the provisional credit without addressing whether the merchants timestamp cutoff was clearly disclosed or whether enforcing it on the XXXX calendar day was consistent with consumer protection standards. 

I am requesting reconsideration of this dispute based on the full facts, including that I acted in good faith within the stated policy window and that the return denial was based on an undisclosed technical cutoff.
